diff --git a/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks-custom.yaml b/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks-custom.yaml
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..bf5f082
--- /dev/null
+++ b/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks-custom.yaml
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# This file shows how to run multi-node training benchmarks using an MPIJob,
+# specifying GPUs explicitly per replica.
+apiVersion: kubeflow.org/v1alpha1
+kind: MPIJob
+metadata:
+  name: tensorflow-benchmarks-16-custom
+spec:
+  replicas: 4
+  template:
+    spec:
+      containers:
+      - image: mpioperator/tensorflow-benchmarks:latest
+        name: tensorflow-benchmarks
+        resources:
+          limits:
+            nvidia.com/gpu: 4
diff --git a/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml b/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml
index 0d29429..68e4b05 100644
--- a/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml
+++ b/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml
@@ -1,4 +1,15 @@
-# This file shows how to run multi-node training benchmarks using an MPIJob.
+# This file shows how to run multi-node training benchmarks using an MPIJob,
+# letting the operator decide on how best to allocate GPUs.
+#
+# In this mode, the operator assumes all nodes have the same number of GPUs.
+# If `gpus` is bigger than the number of GPUs per node, then only whole nodes
+# can be allocated.
+#
+# For example, if each node has 8 GPUs, the valid `gpus` values are:
+# 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 24, 32, ...or any multiple of 8.
+#
+# If you need more flexibility in allocating GPUs, you can use the alternative
+# mode to specify `replicas` and GPU resource limit explicitly.
 apiVersion: kubeflow.org/v1alpha1
 kind: MPIJob
 metadata:

 	GPUs *int32 `json:"gpus,omitempty"`
 
+	// Specifies the desired number of replicas the MPIJob should run on.
+	// The `PodSpec` should specify the number of GPUs.
+	// Mutually exclusive with the `GPUs` field.
+	// +optional
+	Replicas *int32 `json:"replicas,omitempty"`
+
 	// Describes the pod that will be created when executing an MPIJob.

 }

+		in, out := &in.Replicas, &out.Replicas
+		if *in == nil {
+			*out = nil
+		} else {
+			*out = new(int32)
+			**out = **in

+	}
 	in.Template.DeepCopyInto(&out.Template)

 }

message: Use docker hub to automatically build docker images after every commit (#13)
patch:
commit ac101d8135d097416efa14ad06cadd67e374fa44
Author: Rong Ou <rong.ou@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Jun 6 10:33:25 2018 -0700

    Use docker hub to automatically build docker images after every commit (#13)
    
    * Make it possible to do automated builds on docker hub.
    
    * use the docker hub auto built images
---
 cmd/mpi-operator/Dockerfile => Dockerfile |  0
 README.md                                 | 28 ----------------------------
 cmd/kubectl-delivery/Dockerfile           |  6 +-----
 deploy/3-mpi-operator.yaml                |  4 ++--
 examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml       |  2 +-
 5 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 36 deletions(-)

diff --git a/cmd/mpi-operator/Dockerfile b/Dockerfile
similarity index 100%
rename from cmd/mpi-operator/Dockerfile
rename to Dockerfile
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 7960fda..7b95284 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -2,28 +2,6 @@
 
 The MPI Operator makes it easy to run allreduce-style distributed training.
 
-## Build
-
-Check out the code:
-```shell
-mkdir -p ${GOPATH}/src/github.com/kubeflow
-cd ${GOPATH}/src/github.com/kubeflow
-git clone https://github.com/kubeflow/mpi-operator.git
-cd mpi-operator
-```
-
-Build and push the `mpi-operator` Docker image:
-```shell
-docker built -t rongou/mpi-operator:0.1.0 -f cmd/mpi-operator/Dockerfile .
-docker push rongou/mpi-operator:0.1.0
-```
-
-Build and push the `kubectl-delivery` Docker image:
-```shell
-docker build -t rongou/kubectl-delivery:0.1.0 -f cmd/kubectl-delivery/Dockerfile .
-docker push rongou/mpi-operator:0.1.0
-```
-
 ## Deploy
 
 ```shell
@@ -32,12 +10,6 @@ kubectl create -f deploy/
 
 ## Test
 
-Build and push the `tensorflow_benchmarks` Docker image:
-```shell
-docker build -t rongou/tensorflow_benchmarks examples/tensorflow-benchmarks
-docker push rongou/tensorflow_benchmarks
-```
-
 Launch a multi-node tensorflow benchmark training job:
 ```shell
 kubectl create -f examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml
diff --git a/cmd/kubectl-delivery/Dockerfile b/cmd/kubectl-delivery/Dockerfile
index eb1d915..3a58f24 100644
--- a/cmd/kubectl-delivery/Dockerfile
+++ b/cmd/kubectl-delivery/Dockerfile
@@ -7,11 +7,7 @@ RUN wget -q https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/${K8S_VERS
 RUN chmod +x ./kubectl
 RUN mv ./kubectl /bin/kubectl
 
-# Copy all project.
-# This layer is rebuilt when ever a file has changed in the project directory.
-COPY . /go/src/github.com/kubeflow/mpi-operator/
-
 FROM alpine:3.7
 COPY --from=build /bin/kubectl /bin/kubectl
-COPY --from=build /go/src/github.com/kubeflow/mpi-operator/cmd/kubectl-delivery/deliver_kubectl.sh .
+COPY deliver_kubectl.sh .
 ENTRYPOINT ["./deliver_kubectl.sh"]
diff --git a/deploy/3-mpi-operator.yaml b/deploy/3-mpi-operator.yaml
index 6cbb140..754923b 100644
--- a/deploy/3-mpi-operator.yaml
+++ b/deploy/3-mpi-operator.yaml
@@ -18,10 +18,10 @@ spec:
       serviceAccountName: mpi-operator
       containers:
       - name: mpi-operator
-        image: rongou/mpi-operator:0.1.0
+        image: mpioperator/mpi-operator:latest
         args: [
           "--gpus-per-node", "8",
           "--kubectl-delivery-image",
-          "rongou/kubectl-delivery:0.1.0"
+          "mpioperator/kubectl-delivery:latest"
         ]
         imagePullPolicy: Always
diff --git a/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml b/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml
index 6d4ce5c..0d29429 100644
--- a/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml
+++ b/examples/tensorflow-benchmarks.yaml
@@ -8,5 +8,5 @@ spec:
   template:
     spec:
       containers:
-      - image: rongou/tensorflow_benchmarks:latest
+      - image: mpioperator/tensorflow-benchmarks:latest
         name: tensorflow-benchmarks
